# arena-alligator

Repository: https://github.com/tempusfrangit/arena-alligator
Crate: https://crates.io/crates/arena-alligator
Docs: https://docs.rs/arena-alligator
DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/tempusfrangit/arena-alligator
Version: 0.6.0
License: MIT
Rust edition: 2024
MSRV: 1.88

Summary:
arena-alligator is an arena allocator for building bytes::Bytes without copying the final payload.
Write into a Buffer, call freeze(), and hand off Bytes backed by arena memory. The slot or block
returns to the arena when the last clone or slice drops.

Recommended path:
Use FixedArena by default. It is the high-throughput path for workloads that fit one slot size, or
a small set of predictable slot sizes across separate arenas. FixedArena uses uniform slots and a
bitmap claim/release path.

Variable-size path:
Use BuddyArena when variable-size allocation is the requirement. BuddyArena rounds requests up to
powers of two, splits larger blocks on demand, and coalesces neighbors on release. BuddyGeometry
provides exact() and nearest() constructors for shaping the shared region.
- exact(): strict validation, no adjustment
- nearest(): snap total size and minimum block size up to a valid buddy geometry

Core APIs:
- FixedArena::with_slot_capacity(...)
- FixedArena::with_arena_capacity(...)
- BuddyArena::builder(BuddyGeometry::exact(...) or BuddyGeometry::nearest(...))
- Buffer::freeze()
- FixedArenaBuilder::auto_spill()
- FixedArenaBuilder::init_policy(...) and BuddyArenaBuilder::init_policy(...)
- BytesExt::into_owned()
- AsyncFixedArena::allocate_async() and AsyncBuddyArena::allocate_async(...) with feature "async-alloc"
- FixedArena::metrics() and BuddyArena::metrics()

Memory and overflow behavior:
- Default initialization policy is InitPolicy::Uninit. This matches Rust's common writable
  uninitialized-capacity model: newly allocated arena bytes are not zero-filled automatically, and
  only written bytes become visible in frozen Bytes.
- InitPolicy::Zero is available for security-sensitive or data-hygiene-sensitive workloads that
  require zero-on-allocate behavior.
- Writing past capacity panics by default. That follows the contract of a fixed-size BufMut.
- auto_spill() opts into different overflow behavior by copying written data to heap-backed storage
  and releasing the arena allocation immediately.
- BytesExt::into_owned() is the explicit post-freeze handoff from arena-backed Bytes to owned
  mutable heap storage.

NUMA and page faulting:
- One arena per NUMA node is the baseline deployment pattern on multi-socket machines.
- PageSize and build_unfaulted()/fault_pages() exist for explicit page-fault placement control.
- docs/numa.md covers per-node arenas, pinning, and bounded cross-node fallback.

Validation signals:
- Standard unit tests and integration tests
- Doctests on public API entry points
- Example builds and runs in CI
- Miri for undefined-behavior regression checks
- Loom for concurrency/interleaving validation
- Clippy, rustfmt, docs, and MSRV coverage in CI

Development workflow:
This repository uses mise as its task runner.
- Install guide: https://mise.jdx.dev/installing-mise.html
- Discover tasks: mise tasks --all
- Full local validation: mise run ci
- Full tests: mise run test
- Examples: mise run examples
- Benchmarks: mise run bench and mise run bench:extreme
- llms.txt is maintained as a tracked static file in the repository.

Release notes:
- CHANGELOG.md covers 0.3.0, 0.4.0, 0.5.x, and 0.6.0

Related files:
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md
- docs/numa.md
- docs/benchmarks.md
- CONTRIBUTING.md
